decisions.reasoning: MSFT entered bear market territory (-24% in 2026, -17% in June — worst month since 2000) driven by AI CapEx concerns ($30.88B/quarter, +84% YoY) and OpenAI investment losses ($3.1B in Q1 FY26). However, analyst consensus remains Strong Buy (52/55) with avg PT $562, implying 52%+ upside. EU antitrust probe and securities class actions re: Copilot adoption are overhang. Fundamentals (Azure growth, Copilot at scale) intact. Selling here crystallizes a 13% loss into a sentiment-driven selloff. Holding pending macro clarity.

sources.headline: Microsoft Forecast: Strong Buy With Near-Unanimous Analyst Support
Why Microsoft Stock Is Falling: AI Capex and OpenAI Risk
MSFT Stock Enters Bear Market After 21% Drop From Peak: Retail Cautious But Analysts Stay Overwhelmingly Bullish

decisions.invalidation: Azure revenue growth falls below 35% YoY for two consecutive quarters, or CapEx guidance increases again without proportional revenue lift, or OpenAI publicly announces multi-cloud shift away from Azure

decisions.reasoning: NVDA posted exceptional Q1 FY27 (revenue $81.6B, +85% YoY; data center $75.2B). AI demand robust with hyperscaler CapEx projected at $1T in 2027. Export restriction headwind is real: DOC closed Blackwell/B200 loophole for China offshore subsidiaries, JPMorgan/Bernstein estimate $5.5-16B revenue impact. B200 GPU lease rates fell from $6.11 to $4.22 (June 21), signaling supply expansion and margin risk. Despite headwinds, 36/37 analysts rate Buy with consensus PT ~$309 (~58% upside from $195). Technical signal is flat. Holding; not adding into export overhang.

sources.headline: NVDA Stock Slips As U.S. Tightens China AI Chip Loophole
NVDA Stock Forecast 2026: Analyst Predictions - consensus PT $309
Why Is NVIDIA Stock Falling? China, AI Demand & Valuation

decisions.invalidation: Further export restrictions broaden to Hopper/Grace chips, or B200 GPU lease rates fall below $3.50 (margin collapse signal), or next quarterly revenue misses consensus by >10%

decisions.reasoning: QQQ is the only position with a bullish SMA crossover signal (fast $724 > slow $722). However, short-term tech sector headwinds (AI CapEx concerns, export restrictions) and pending macro data (nonfarm payrolls, CPI) argue for patience. Minor loss (-2.63%). Holding for diversification and pending clearer macro signals.

decisions.invalidation: Broad market breaks below 200-day MA, or CPI data shows inflation re-acceleration

decisions.reasoning: Only position in the green (+0.97%). Economy expanding solidly, S&P 500 earnings growth projected at 25% for 2026 (per Schwab mid-year outlook). Fed held rates at 3.5-3.75% at June 17 FOMC. Sticky inflation remains risk. Holding as broad market anchor.

decisions.invalidation: Recession signals (2 consecutive months of negative job growth), or Fed hikes more than 50bps in H2 2026

decisions.reasoning: Preserving $853.46 (9.2% of equity) as dry powder. Rationale: market awaiting nonfarm payrolls and CPI data that could create volatility and better entry points. Tech sector under broad selling pressure. No compelling new buys identified without accepting excessive risk into existing headwinds.
decisions.invalidation: Clear macro catalyst (strong jobs report + CPI below 2.5%) combined with technical breakout in QQQ above 30-day SMA

summary: First session — hold all positions (MSFT, NVDA, QQQ, SPY), preserve 9.2% cash; no trades given tech AI-CapEx headwinds, export restrictions, and pending macro data
open_theses: MSFT: Azure + Copilot long-term monetization intact despite severe June selloff; awaiting stabilization before adding
NVDA: AI GPU demand structural despite export/lease-rate headwinds; 36/37 analyst Buy consensus; watch B200 pricing floor
CASH: Dry powder for better entries post-CPI/jobs data week
